def test_hello_file_content():
        """Test that out.txt contains exactly the flag."""
        hello_path = Path("/app/out.txt")
    
>       assert hello_path.read_text().strip() == "flag{gc0d3_iz_ch4LLenGiNg}", (

        )
E       AssertionError: Expected the correct flag but got 'flag{gcOd3_iz_ch4LLenGiNg}
E         
E         The text.gcode file prints a single line of embossed text.
E         When printed, the text reads (leetspeak for "gcode is challenging"):

E             flag{gcOd3_iz_ch4LLenGiNg}
E         
E         How it was decoded:
E         - Object 0 in the file is named "Embossed text" (object 1 is "Shape-Box").
E         - Its extrusion moves span ~168 mm x ~12 mm but lie on a plane that is
E           tilted relative to the X/Y/Z axes, so plain top/front/side projections
E           only show bold blobs or infill lines.
E         - Fitting the object's principal plane (3D PCA) and projecting the glyph
E           outlines onto it makes the lettering legible. Only this orientation
E           yields readable text; the flipped/mirrored variants are gibberish.'
